message:
refactor: concurrent agent improvements to ensemble and CLI

Updates from concurrent agent development:
- internal/cli/ensemble.go: Ensemble CLI improvements
- internal/cli/grep.go: Grep command enhancements
- internal/ensemble/checkpoint.go: Checkpoint handling
- internal/ensemble/synthesizer.go: Synthesizer improvements
- internal/robot/pagination.go: Pagination refinements
- Related test updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1iznv","title":"Task: Implement mode badges, tier chips, and colors","description":"# Task: Implement mode badges, tier chips, and colors

## Purpose
Visual distinction for reasoning modes in dashboard and synthesizer views.

## Scope
- Mode badge: short code (A1, F43) + icon
- Tier chip: CORE / ADV / EXP with subtle color palette
- Category color: map A–L to a consistent palette
- Status color: pending/active/done overlays

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/ui_styles.go
func ModeBadge(mode ReasoningMode) string
func TierChip(tier ModeTier) string
func CategoryColor(cat ModeCategory) lipgloss.Color
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mode badge includes code + icon
- [ ] Tier chip shows CORE/ADV/EXP
- [ ] Colors consistent across dashboard + synthesizer
- [ ] Works in ASCII fallback mode

## Dependencies
- Ensemble panel (bd-121pa)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:38.468762804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:59:53.454493698Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:59:53.454474602Z","close_reason":"Added mode/tier UI helpers and hooked in dashboard","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iznv","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1j956","title":"RCH: Implement DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands","description":"Ensure RCH-related commands are whitelisted in DCG.

## Background

RCH intercepts build commands and may transform them:
- Original: `cargo build`
- Transformed: `rch build cargo -- build`

DCG might block rch commands if not whitelisted.

## Integration Points

### 1. Whitelist RCH Commands
Add to DCG whitelist:
```
rch *             # All rch commands
rch build *       # Build commands
rch intercept *   # Intercept invocations
```

### 2. Pass-Through Logic
When DCG encounters an RCH-wrapped command, check inner command against blocklist.

### 3. Code Location
- internal/integrations/coordinator.go
- Document in DCG config section

## Acceptance Criteria

1. DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands
2. rch build and rch offload whitelisted
3. Inner command validation works
4. Config option to enable/disable integration
5. Unit tests for whitelist matching
6. E2E test verifying RCH commands not blocked","acceptance_criteria":"1. DCG whitelist integration for RCH commands
2. rch build and rch offload whitelisted
3. Config option to enable/disable integration
4. Unit tests for whitelist matching
5.  {"id":"bd-1jiuu","title":"DCG: Implement audit logging for blocked commands","description":"Log all blocked commands to audit file for review and debugging.

## Requirements

1. Log every blocked command with:
   - Timestamp (RFC3339)
   - Command attempted
   - Agent/pane that attempted it
   - DCG rule that triggered block
   - Session ID

2. Log format: JSONL (one JSON object per line)

3. Rotation: rotate when file exceeds 10MB

## Log Entry Schema

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "event": "command_blocked",
  "command": "rm -rf /important",
  "pane": "agent-1",
  "session": "my-project",
  "rule": "rm_recursive_dangerous",
  "dcg_output": "Blocked: rm -rf with dangerous path"
}
```

## Implementation

```go
package dcg

type AuditLogger struct {
    path     string
    file     *os.File
    mu       sync.Mutex
    maxBytes int64
}

func (l *AuditLogger) LogBlocked(cmd string, pane string, rule string, output string) error {
    // Write JSONL entry
    // Check rotation
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/dcg/audit.go

## Testing
- Log blocked command, verify entry written
- Fill log to rotation threshold, verify new file created
- Concurrent logging (multiple agents blocking simultaneously)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:29.829975966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:27.591217606Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:16:27.591053347Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jiuu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1jiuu","depends_on_id":"bd-o97j9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1k3ga","title":"Tier 2: Performance Optimizations","description":"EPIC for Tier 2 integrations focused on performance improvements.

## Why Tier 2 is Second Priority

After safety and reliability (Tier 1), performance becomes the next concern:

1. **TOON**: Token costs are a major expense in multi-agent workflows. Robot mode already outputs JSON for every command. TOON (Token-Optimized Object Notation) can reduce this by 40-60%, saving significant money over time.

2. **RCH**: When multiple agents run builds simultaneously, the host machine becomes CPU-bound. RCH offloads compilation to remote workers, keeping the local machine responsive for agent interactions.

## Integration Approach

Both are Rust projects that work via subprocess invocation:
- TOON: Replace JSON serialization with TOON format in robot mode output
- RCH: Hook into cargo/go build commands via PreToolUse

## Success Criteria

1. TOON integration achieves 40-60% token reduction
2. All robot commands support TOON output format
3. RCH offloads builds transparently
4. Worker health monitoring works
5. Config sections for both integrations
6. All components have unit tests (80% coverage)
7. E2E tests verify performance improvements
8. Documentation covers configuration and usage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:01:30.314902828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:01:42.002097685Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k3ga","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-cpyn8","title":"Task: Write E2E test for full ensemble pipeline (intake → modes → synthesis)","description":"End-to-end test covering the complete 3-stage pipeline with real tmux.

## Test Scope
Full pipeline execution from CLI to synthesis report.

## Test Cases

### E2E: Happy Path - project-diagnosis ensemble
```go
// e2e/ensemble_pipeline_test.go
func TestE2E_Ensemble_ProjectDiagnosis_FullPipeline(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: Create test project with known issues
    // Stage 1: Verify context pack generation
    // Stage 2: Verify mode agents spawn and complete
    // Stage 3: Verify synthesis produces valid report
    // Teardown: Clean up tmux sessions
}
```

### E2E: Budget Enforcement
```go
func TestE2E_Ensemble_BudgetEnforcement(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: Set low token budget
    // Execute: Run ensemble with budget constraints
    // Verify: Agents stopped at cap, partial synthesis occurred
}
```

### E2E: Early Stop Trigger
```go
func TestE2E_Ensemble_EarlyStop(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: Configure aggressive early stop thresholds
    // Execute: Run ensemble with redundant modes
    // Verify: Early stop triggered, metrics recorded
}
```

### E2E: Schema Validation Failure
```go
func TestE2E_Ensemble_SchemaValidationFailure(t *testing.T) {
    // Setup: Mock agent that produces invalid output
    // Execute: Run ensemble
    // Verify: Invalid output flagged, synthesis handles gracefully
}
```

### E2E: Context Pack Caching
```go
func TestE2E_Ensemble_ContextPackCaching(t *testing.T) {
    // Execute: Run ensemble twice on same project
    // Verify: Second run uses cached context pack
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log every stage transition with timestamp
- Log all tmux operations
- Log budget state at each agent completion
- Log schema validation results
- Log synthesis inputs and outputs
- Log metrics (coverage, redundancy, velocity)

## Test Fixtures
- e2e/testdata/sample-project/ - Known-issues project
- e2e/testdata/expected-outputs/ - Golden synthesis outputs

## Timeout Handling
- Individual agent timeout: 60s
- Total pipeline timeout: 5 minutes
- Graceful cleanup on timeout

 {"id":"bd-cqnm8","title":"Implement targeting examples generation","description":"Implement targeting examples generation.

## Fields

- pane_format: 'session:window.pane'
- example_agent_pane: e.g., 'myproject:1.2'
- example_control_pane: e.g., 'myproject:1.1'

## Purpose

Provide copy-paste ready examples for correct pane targeting.
Agents can use these examples as templates.

## Generation Logic

```go
func generateTargetingExamples(session string, windowIdx, ctrlPane, firstAgent int) {
    return TargetingExamples{
        PaneFormat: "session:window.pane",
        ExampleAgentPane: fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d.%d", session, windowIdx, firstAgent),
        ExampleControlPane: fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d.%d", session, windowIdx, ctrlPane),
    }
}
```

## Additional Examples

Consider adding:
- All agent panes list
- Range notation (e.g., 2-16)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:29:49.638785959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:30.029965370Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:30.029875501Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cqnm8","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-cs5x4","title":"Task: WebSocket event persistence + resume + backpressure","description":"## Scope\nAdd event retention with cursor resume, **SQLite persistence**, and backpressure handling.\n\n## Implementation\n- Per-topic ring buffer with seq numbers.\n- Persist recent events to SQLite (bounded retention).\n- Cursor model: since cursor or seq; supports replay after reconnect.\n- stream.reset event with snapshot when cursor expired.\n- Subscription options: throttle_ms, max_lines_per_msg, mode lines/raw.\n\n## Logging\n- Log dropped events, cursor resets, buffer overflows, and retention trims.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: ring buffer order + cursor resume.\n- Integration: persist/replay from SQLite after restart.\n- WebSocket: backpressure triggers pane.output.dropped events.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Resume works reliably for short outages and after restart.\n- Dropped output is surfaced to clients with counts.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"claude-opus-4.5","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:51:50.618348618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:23:24.241508343Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:23:24.241185584Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cs5x4","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-d9jwi","title":"caut: Add [integrations.caut] config section to TOML","description":"Add configuration support for caut integration in NTM config file.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.caut]
# Enable caut usage tracking integration
enabled = true

# Path to caut binary (optional)
binary_path = ""

# Polling interval in seconds
poll_interval = 60

# Alert threshold (percentage)
alert_threshold = 80

# Providers to track (empty = all)
providers = []

# Enable per-agent attribution (if supported)
per_agent_tracking = true

# Cost display currency
currency = "USD"
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go (add CautConfig struct)
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go

## Struct Definition

```go
type CautConfig struct {
    Enabled          bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath       string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    PollInterval     int      `toml:"poll_interval"`
    AlertThreshold   int      `toml:"alert_threshold"`
    Providers        []string `toml:"providers"`
    PerAgentTracking bool     `toml:"per_agent_tracking"`
    Currency         string   `toml:"currency"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:58:23.362748020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:32:52.487804704Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:32:52.487760010Z","close_reason":"Added CautConfig struct with poll_interval, alert_threshold, providers, per_agent_tracking, and currency options","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-d9jwi","depends_on_id":"bd-2lsyy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dgag","title":"E2E Tests: Score tracking and recommendations","description":"E2E tests for score tracking and recommendations.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Score Tracking
1. Record synthetic scores for multiple agents
2. Verify scores updated + persisted (JSONL)
3. Check trend window calculations

### Scenario 2: Recommendation Influence
1. Build score history per agent and task type
2. Request recommendations/assignments
3. Verify scores influence order (with explainable rationale)

### Scenario 3: Score Reset / Cold Start
1. Start with empty tracker
2. Verify recommendations return to default behavior
3. Add minimal samples → still gated
4. Add enough samples → scoring kicks in

## Logging
- Log input scores and computed ranking
- Log trend summary and sample counts
- Include session/agent/task/bead IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Score tracking verified across tasks
2. Recommendation order matches scores (when gated)
3. Score persistence works correctly
4. Reset/cold-start behavior tested
5. Test logs include score values
6. Test duration under 5 minutes

 {"id":"bd-dkxa0","title":"Task: Implement ContextPack types and project brief structure","description":"Define core types for the context pack system.

## Types
```go
// internal/ensemble/context.go
type ContextPack struct {
    Hash            string         // For cache keying
    GeneratedAt     time.Time
    ProjectBrief    *ProjectBrief
    UserContext     *UserContext
    TokenEstimate   int            // Estimated tokens when injected
}

type ProjectBrief struct {
    Name            string
    Description     string         // From README
    Languages       []string       // Detected
    Frameworks      []string       // From dependencies
    Structure       *ProjectStructure
    RecentActivity  []CommitSummary
    OpenIssues      int            // From .beads
}

type ProjectStructure struct {
    EntryPoints     []string
    CorePackages    []string
    TestCoverage    float64
    TotalFiles      int
    TotalLines      int
}

type UserContext struct {
    ProblemStatement string
    FocusAreas       []string
    Constraints      []string
    Stakeholders     []string
}
```
